perturbation.original_text: MMMW will provide on-call service for the Technical Infrastructure during the term of this Agreement from 7:00am to 5:00pm CST, with a guaranteed response time of 3 hours with a goal of on site within 2 hours. Support to include 7X24 hour pager support for emergency services.
perturbation.changed_text: MMMW will provide on-call service for the Technical Infrastructure during the term of this Agreement during normal business hours. Support availability outside of these hours is subject to additional fees.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the specific hours (7:00am to 5:00pm CST) and the guaranteed response time of 3 hours, and removing the '7X24 hour pager support for emergency services', the changed text introduces ambiguity regarding the scope of on-call service and emergency response. This contradicts the overall intention of providing reliable technical support, creating an uncertainty about the level of support MMMW is obligated to provide.
perturbation.contradicted_text: MMMW will provide on-call service for the Technical Infrastructure during the term of this Agreement during normal business hours. Support availability outside of these hours is subject to additional fees. After the customer has 30 locations, MMMW will locate technical personnel in the current geography. If a system is not able to be on-line after hours, on-site help will be made available.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly alters the original agreement regarding on-call service by removing specific hours and guaranteed response times. The contradicted text, which appears identical to the changed text, now lacks the clarity provided in the original text concerning the scope and availability of on-call support. This creates a contradiction because the changed/contradicted text states that support availability outside of normal business hours is subject to additional fees, but the original text and another part of the document specify 7X24 hour pager support for emergency services, without mentioning additional fees, thus making the terms inconsistent and contradictory.

perturbation.original_text: Charges will be invoiced and are payable within thirty (30) days after the date of the invoice. All charges are subject to change by MMMW for the forthcoming period capped at the CPI annually, on thirty days written notice. MMMW reserves the right to apply [*] finance charges.
perturbation.changed_text: Charges will be invoiced and are payable as per MMMW's standard payment terms. All charges are subject to change by MMMW on thirty days written notice.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'within thirty (30) days after the date of the invoice' and 'capped at the CPI annually', the modified text creates uncertainty about the payment timeframe and the limits on charge increases. This omission creates an in-text contradiction because the previous version had a clear deadline which now contradicts 'MMMW's standard payment terms,' as the exact timeframe for payment is left unspecified and potentially open to arbitrary changes, impacting financial predictability for the Customer.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Charges will be invoiced and are payable as per MMMW's standard payment terms. All charges are subject to change by MMMW on thirty days written notice. MMMW reserves the right to apply [*] finance charges.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specific payment timeframe ('within thirty (30) days after the date of the invoice') and the limit on charge increases ('capped at the CPI annually'). This omission creates an in-text contradiction with the contradicted text ('Charges will be invoiced and are payable as per MMMW's standard payment terms'). The contradicted text refers to MMMW's standard payment terms, but without the original timeframe and cap, the payment terms become ambiguous and potentially conflict with the customer's understanding or expectations, creating uncertainty and impacting financial predictability.

perturbation.original_text: MMMW warrants that the Technical Infrastructure remains in satisfactory operating condition provided it is: 1) continuously subject to MMMW's inspection and acceptance of the hardware for service; and 2) subject to normal use and conditions. MMMW's sole and exclusive obligation under this warranty shall be at its option to repair or exchange any hardware not in satisfactory operating condition. Said obligation shall be subject to the conditions and charges of Section 3, 4 and 5 and the prompt submission of (or notification to MMMW of the problem) the hardware to MMMW for service.
perturbation.changed_text: MMMW warrants that the Technical Infrastructure remains in satisfactory operating condition provided it is subject to normal use and conditions. MMMW's obligation under this warranty shall be to provide support. Said obligation shall be subject to the conditions of Section 3, 4 and 5 and the notification to MMMW of the problem.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting '1) continuously subject to MMMW's inspection and acceptance of the hardware for service;' and 'at its option to repair or exchange any hardware not in satisfactory operating condition.', and replacing them with 'provide support', the changed text weakens the warranty. This introduces ambiguity as to what 'provide support' entails. The omission contradicts the previous specification of 'repair or exchange', making the warranty terms vague and unenforceable, and potentially leading to disputes regarding the level of service expected versus the actual service provided.
perturbation.contradicted_text: MMMW warrants that the Technical Infrastructure remains in satisfactory operating condition provided it is subject to normal use and conditions. MMMW's obligation under this warranty shall be to provide support. Said obligation shall be subject to the conditions of Section 3, 4 and 5 and the notification to MMMW of the problem.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the warranty by omitting the requirement for 'MMMW's inspection and acceptance' and the explicit obligation to 'repair or exchange any hardware'. Replacing these with 'provide support' creates ambiguity and contradicts the original, more specific warranty terms, as it is unclear what 'support' entails, thus creating an in-text contradiction. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition, as essential language was removed, creating a contradiction with obligations stated in the contradicted section (which is identical to the changed text but contrasts with the original text from which it was derived).
